findings.impact: The PR description says existing JSON behavior stays intact, but the changed default flips no-flag invocations from machine-readable JSON to markdown output.
findings.test_gap: There is no regression coverage for the default no-flag path even though automation relies on the historical JSON default.
findings.title: Changing the CLI default to markdown violates the stated JSON-compat contract

findings.impact: Even callers that pass --output json will get a markdown string back because the registry binds the json key to render_markdown.
findings.test_gap: The new tests only exercise markdown snapshots, so the explicit JSON path is no longer protected.
findings.title: The renderer registry now routes explicit json requests to markdown

findings.impact: The test delta locks in the new markdown happy path but never checks either the no-flag default or explicit json behavior that the PR claims to preserve.
findings.test_gap: Add one test for the default invocation and one for explicit --output json so the contract drift is caught.
findings.title: The added coverage only snapshots markdown and misses the two JSON regression paths

summary: The PR does not match its stated intent: it changes the default output away from JSON, breaks explicit json rendering via the registry, and only adds markdown-path coverage. The release context makes the default-path change more severe because the nightly consumer shells out without flags.
